contents.value: **fun** — ATS/Postiats keyword.
**fn** — ATS/Postiats keyword.
**val** — ATS/Postiats keyword.
**var** — ATS/Postiats keyword.
**let** — ATS/Postiats keyword.
**println!** — Built-in function/command in ATS/Postiats.
**print!** — Built-in function/command in ATS/Postiats.
**assertloc** — Built-in function/command in ATS/Postiats.
**fprint** — Built-in function/command in ATS/Postiats.
**fprintln** — Built-in function/command in ATS/Postiats.
**match** performs pattern matching against a value.
**type** defines a new type alias or algebraic data type.
A **record** groups named fields into a product type.
A **variant** (sum type) represents one of several possible shapes.
A **function** maps input values to output values. First-class in functional languages.
**Recursion** occurs when a function calls itself to solve smaller subproblems.
**Pattern matching** destructures values and dispatches based on shape.
A **tuple** is a fixed-size product of heterogeneous values.
**Immutable** values cannot be changed after creation.
A **higher-order function** takes functions as arguments or returns them.
**Currying** transforms a multi-argument function into a sequence of single-argument functions.
A **monad** is an abstraction for sequencing computations with context (e.g., Option, Result).
**fold** (reduce) combines elements of a collection into a single result.
**map** applies a function to each element of a collection, producing a new collection.

```postiats
datatype name = Constructor1 of type | Constructor2 of type
```
Declares an algebraic data type in ATS/Postiats with named constructors for pattern matching. Datatypes are garbage-collected and support polymorphism through type parameters.

```postiats
datavtype name = Constructor1 of type | Constructor2 of type
```
Declares a linear algebraic data type (viewtype) in ATS/Postiats that must be explicitly freed. Linear datatypes enable safe manual memory management enforced by the type system.

```postiats
sortdef name = sort_expression
```
Defines a named sort abbreviation in the statics of ATS/Postiats. Sorts classify types and are used in dependent type specifications to constrain static indices for verified programming.

```postiats
implement name(params) = body
```
Provides the implementation for a previously declared function template or interface function in ATS/Postiats, separating specification from implementation for modular program design.

```postiats
stadef name = static_expression
```
Defines a static abbreviation in ATS/Postiats for use in dependent types and sort expressions. Static definitions enable reusable type-level computations and index expressions.

```postiats
typedef name = type_expression
```
Creates a type alias in ATS/Postiats, providing a shorter name for a complex type expression. Type definitions improve readability and enable reuse of common type patterns.

```postiats
prval name: prop = proof_expression
```
Declares a proof value in ATS/Postiats that exists only at compile time for theorem proving. Proof values are erased during compilation and have zero runtime cost.

```postiats
prfun name(proof_params): prop
```
Declares a proof function in ATS/Postiats that transforms proofs at compile time. Proof functions witness logical implications and are entirely erased from the generated executable code.

```postiats
let declarations in expression end
```
A scoping construct in ATS/Postiats that introduces local declarations visible only within the expression between in and end. Enables structured local bindings with controlled visibility.

```postiats
case expression of pattern1 => result1 | pattern2 => result2
```
Pattern matching expression in ATS/Postiats that deconstructs values against patterns and executes the first matching branch. The compiler checks exhaustiveness to prevent unhandled cases.

```postiats
String
```
Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

```postiats
Number
```
Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

```postiats
Boolean
```
Logical type with two values: true and false.

```postiats
Array
```
Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

```postiats
Object
```
Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

```postiats
null
```
Represents the intentional absence of any value.

```postiats
undefined
```
Represents an uninitialized or missing value.

```postiats
print(value)
```
Output a value to the standard output stream.
